user_message: Can you pull the context on Fourth Coffee? I need to run the hygiene check.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 15:34 EST
Subject: Follow-up: consumption trend Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-01-11 09:28 EST
Subject: Re: consumption trend Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for consumption trend. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Fourth Coffee
Date: 2024-02-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: David Rivera (Fourth Coffee), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:08:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:11:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:19:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:22:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
